intro: Sieversia pusilla, also known as Sievers' grass or dwarf Sieversia, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Sieversia pusilla include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Sieversia pusilla is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and digestive issues.
